preview_text:
The model ranks Argentina as a strong favorite at 76%, leaving Austria with only a 4% chance of victory. The gap reflects a substantial disparity in the forecast; Austria's path to three points appears unlikely under the statistical framework, though not impossible.

The draw carries meaningful probability at 20%, suggesting the match could prove competitive despite Argentina's advantage. The W/D/L spread—76/20/4—indicates a one-sided contest in expectation, yet a quarter of outcomes would avoid decisive resolution. Austria's low win probability does not preclude a tightly contested or goalless result.

Model and market diverge meaningfully here. The sportsbook consensus credits Argentina at 61%, some 15 percentage points below the model, while assigning Austria 15%—more than triple the model's 4%. Polymarket sits between the two. The dashboard does not adjudicate which assessment is more accurate; the gap warrants attention from those seeking value.
